_comment_top: R6 Pillar 3 / Q11 / task 021 — seed row for the generic InvokePlaybookHandler chat tool. Surfaces a single LLM-visible 'invoke_playbook(playbookId, parameters)' function that dispatches to ANY tenant-accessible playbook via the IInvokePlaybookAi facade (task 020). Replaces the specialized InvokeSummarizePlaybookTool + InvokeInsightsQueryTool bridges (deleted in Wave 10 / task 023).
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'InvokePlaybookHandler' (= nameof handler class). Auto-discovered via ToolFrameworkExtensions.AddToolHandlersFromAssembly (ADR-010) — ZERO new top-level DI line.
_comment_facade_boundary: ADR-013 binding: the handler injects IInvokePlaybookAi ONLY (the public facade). NEVER IPlaybookOrchestrationService, IPlaybookExecutionEngine, or any AI-internal type. The chat-tool dispatch path is a CRUD-side caller of the orchestration layer; the facade is the canonical bridge per project CLAUDE.md §MUST NOT.
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = null (intentional). This dispatcher is the generic mechanism available to ALL playbooks; per-playbook authorization is enforced INSIDE the orchestration layer the facade wraps + by the handler's own tenant-visibility check (sprk_analysisplaybook row lookup via IPlaybookService). Standalone chat (no playbook) sees the tool but every dispatch is validated against the tenant's accessible playbook set — there is no information leakage about cross-tenant playbook existence (the handler returns a uniform ValidationFailed for both 'not found' and 'no access').
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool. Task 022 (next) makes this description dynamic — appending the list of currently-active playbooks the tenant has access to. For now we ship the static text below.
_comment_parameters_schema: The parameters: object additionalProperties: true contract is intentional. Per-playbook parameter schema validation happens INSIDE the handler against the playbook's own ConfigJson field (the playbook row may declare a parameter schema on its sprk_configjson). We CANNOT pre-declare the union of all playbook parameter shapes at the seed-row level — the row is generic across every playbook in the tenant. additionalProperties: true allows the LLM to pass playbook-specific keys without adapter pre-rejection.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass with sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Invoke any registered playbook by ID with parameters. Use this when the user requests an analysis already configured as a playbook (summarization, extraction, comparison, prefill, etc.). The playbook executes its full node graph and returns aggregated text, structured data, and citations. Pass the playbook GUID as 'playbookId' and any template-substitution variables as a 'parameters' JSON object.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat only. The generic playbook dispatcher does NOT support playbook-context invocation (playbooks don't chain to other playbooks via chat-tool indirection). Future R7+ work MAY extend this to Both = 100000002 if cross-playbook composition becomes a requirement.

sprk_jsonschema.title: InvokePlaybookHandler Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the generic invoke_playbook tool. 'playbookId' identifies the playbook to dispatch; 'parameters' carries optional template-substitution variables the playbook's nodes consume.

playbookId.description: GUID of the playbook to invoke (sprk_analysisplaybook row ID). Validated against tenant-accessible playbooks before dispatch — invalid or cross-tenant IDs are rejected with a uniform ValidationFailed response.

parameters.description: Optional caller-supplied parameter map for template substitution in playbook node prompts. Keys + values are deterministic identifiers / display values — ADR-015 binding: NEVER raw user message text. Per-playbook schema validation happens inside the handler against the playbook's own configuration.

sprk_configuration._comment: No method-discriminator dispatch — this row exposes a single LLM-facing function. Empty object reserved for future per-row defaults (e.g., per-tenant timeout override, audit-tag forwarding).
